items.correct: Producing a specific quantity of identical items, then switching to make another batch.
items.why: Students confuse batch with mass production. Batch produces FIXED QUANTITIES with the line then changing - between one-off (single item) and mass (continuous high volume).

items.q: What is the difference between CAD and CAM?
items.correct: CAD creates digital designs; CAM uses those designs to control manufacturing machines.
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: CAD is for cars, CAM is for cameras.
items.why: Students treat the terms as synonymous because they often appear together. CAD = drawing/modelling software; CAM = machine control (CNC routers, 3D printers) - typically the second stage.

items.q: What does 'Rethink' mean in the 6 Rs?
items.correct: Questioning whether a product is necessary or could be designed differently for sustainability.
items.misconception: Trying a design again if it does not work first time.
items.why: Students apply 'rethink' to design iteration. In sustainability, it means challenging the NEED for a product altogether, or fundamentally rethinking how it's made - a more radical step than reducing materials.

items.q: Oak is which type of timber?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Hardwood. “Softwood” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Medium Density Fibreboard. “Multi-Drawer Frame” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Typical safe moisture content (%) for interior timber?
items.why: The real figure is 10 %. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
